tax: Placed at family rank, Ferrous Metalkin is defined by composition and formation mechanics, not by colour, size, or danger: a precipitated or massive iron body fed by iron bog or ore. A new genus belongs here only if it shows a ferrous body with its own magnetic mass-mode.
ap: The grouping is recognised by a rust-red magnetic mass dragging iron toward it. A field surveyor confirms membership from at least one persistent marker — a rust-bleed trail and clinging iron filings — which survives even when the body disperses.
eco: Ferrous Metalkin occupies iron-stained bogs and ore masses. Persistence is elemental rather than reproductive: members form where earth-pressure cements an iron bloom and recur with the bog re-precipitates iron, sustaining themselves on earth-pressure in iron mud.
beh: Across the grouping, behaviour is the disturbance-response of the medium: poses as a bog-iron bank, rams with magnetic mass. Roles, moods, and summoning circumstances are recorded on the entries below, not treated as new taxa.
traitsText: Shared characters: heavy magnetic body and pressure-sense. These are grouping-level traits only; specific resistances, abilities, and field signs belong to each genus and species beneath this node.
abilities: Grouping-level abilities are the broad capacities implied by the shared body: mass-ram and tool-stripping. Named, bounded powers are assigned to the species that actually possess them.
bg: In the Throat, Ferrous Metalkin matters because disarms crews by dragging off their iron. It lets survey reports sort magnetic mass hazards from conductive ones without collapsing distinct hazards into one vague label.
g: Ferrous Metalkin is the family-rank grouping of precipitated-iron elementals of the bog and ore-mass kind within the True Elementals. Its members share a heavy magnetic ferrous body that attacks by mass, not current, and divide below into genus groups by iron form — porous bog-bloom among them. Inert, magnetic, and crushingly heavy.
